ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'coffee beverage' class (FOODON_03301242); no specific class exists for white coffee or bamboo-salt white coffee. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN000A covers coffee and coffee substitutes as a category. No specific facet for white coffee or bamboo salt variant; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 33635003 represents coffee as a substance; no specific concept for white coffee or bamboo salt white coffee. Closest parent used.

preparation_method: mixed with condensed milk or creamer

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard hot cup of bamboo salt white coffee, approximately 240 ml (240 g), as typically served at a Singaporean café or hawker-style outlet.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, coffee, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA SR Legacy 14209) adapted with condensed milk addition and bamboo salt adjustment; white coffee nutrient profile estimated from USDA FoodData Central coffee with milk entries
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for bamboo salt white coffee exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated by combining: (1) USDA FoodData Central entry for coffee with milk (FDC ID 171890, 'Beverages, coffee, brewed, prepared with tap water') as the coffee base, and (2) USDA FoodData Central entry for sweetened condensed milk (FDC ID 170859) to account for the creamer/condensed milk component typical of Malaysian/Singaporean white coffee. Bamboo salt contributes negligible macronutrients but adds a small sodium increment. The resulting per-100g values reflect a typical 240 ml serving of white coffee with condensed milk diluted to serving volume. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — coffee with milk and sugar analogous estimate; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for bamboo salt white coffee. GI of 41 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for coffee with milk and sugar (GI ~41, low category), which is the closest analogous beverage to Singaporean/Malaysian white coffee prepared with condensed milk. Glycemic load per 240 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (41 × 21.1) / 100 ≈ 8.7, rounded to 9.0. Source: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021. Diabetes Care. 2021;44(5):e53–e74.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from bamboo salt and condensed milk; caffeine-mediated transient blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit intake of bamboo salt white coffee due to its sodium contribution (~91 mg per 240 ml serving) from bamboo salt and the transient blood pressure rise associated with caffeine; limiting to one cup per day and choosing lower-sugar formulations is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content from condensed milk (~19.7 g sugar per 240 ml serving) with moderate glycemic index of 41
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume bamboo salt white coffee with caution due to its significant sugar load from condensed milk (approximately 19.7 g per serving, GL ≈ 9), which can cause postprandial blood glucose elevation; requesting reduced or no condensed milk and monitoring portion frequency is recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat from condensed milk; low cholesterol content per serving
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Bamboo salt white coffee contributes a modest amount of saturated fat (~1.7 g per 240 ml serving) primarily from condensed milk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate frequency of consumption and consider unsweetened or plant-based milk alternatives to reduce saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bamboo Salt White Coffee (Hot) is a specialty hot coffee beverage served at Hai Kah Lang restaurant in Funan Mall, Singapore. It is a variation of the traditional Malaysian-style white coffee (白咖啡), which originated in Ipoh, Malaysia, and is characterised by coffee beans roasted with palm oil margarine rather than sugar or wheat, producing a lighter, less bitter brew. In this variant, bamboo salt (竹盐, zhú yán) — a Korean-origin mineral salt produced by packing sea salt into bamboo tubes and firing them at high temperatures — is incorporated into the beverage, imparting a subtle mineral, slightly alkaline flavour profile that complements the natural sweetness of the condensed milk or non-dairy creamer used in white coffee preparation. The drink is served hot and has a creamy, mellow taste with low bitterness compared to traditional espresso-based coffees. Nutritionally, per 240 ml serving it provides approximately 125 kcal, 3.4 g protein, 2.9 g fat, and 21.1 g carbohydrate, predominantly from the condensed milk component. The sugar content is notable (~19.7 g per serving), making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose or weight. The glycemic index is estimated at 41 (low category), though the glycemic load per serving is approximately 9 due to the carbohydrate volume. Bamboo salt adds a small sodium increment. This beverage is suitable for vegetarians and is generally considered halal. It is not suitable for vegans due to the dairy content from condensed milk. Clinically, the primary concerns are sugar load for diabetic individuals and caffeine-related transient blood pressure effects for hypertensive patients.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for bamboo salt white coffee exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central entries for brewed coffee and sweetened condensed milk, adjusted for typical serving dilution. GI is estimated from the closest analogous beverage (coffee with milk and sugar) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-based estimation methodology is well-grounded in approved sources and the beverage composition is well-understood.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component entries (brewed coffee + condensed milk) as no direct database entry exists for bamboo salt white coffee. Values should be updated if HPB Singapore Foodo or a peer-reviewed compositional analysis of this specific product becomes available. 2. Bamboo salt contributes negligible macronutrients but adds approximately 5–15 mg additional sodium per 100 g depending on the quantity used; the sodium value of 38 mg/100 g reflects this increment. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for the condensed milk and brewed coffee components used in estimation. 4. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — naturally zero as coffee beverages with milk contain no dietary fibre. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a dairy-coffee beverage with no fruit or vegetable components. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entries used for estimation. 7. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entries used for estimation. 8. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entries used for estimation. 9. glycemic_index set to 41 (analogous estimate from coffee with milk and sugar, University of Sydney GI Database); gi_category low. Glycemic load per 240 g serving ≈ 9.0. 10. Multilingual names provided for English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for Singaporean cuisine classification. 11. The food is classified under Singaporean cuisine for multilingual purposes as it is served in Singapore, though white coffee originates from Ipoh, Malaysia. 12. Price SGD 6.38 preserved from input (Hai Kah Lang, Funan Mall, GrabFood Singapore).
